spec_match.rationale: Each .nav-item carries anchor-scope: --item-anchor and each trigger sets anchor-name: --item-anchor — the canonical pattern for a menu where every trigger reuses one name.

interactive.rationale: Real popover-based submenus, native popover API, aria-expanded sync, @supports fallback. Three triggers, three independent popovers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises three triggers sharing --item-anchor with each popover opening beneath its OWN trigger; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Has @supports not (anchor-scope: --x) fallback, scope-status banner reports support, popover toggle handlers wired.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables and design system colours throughout. One smell: .submenu uses `inset-area: block-end span-inline-end` but the sibling v131 feature deprecates inset-area in favour of position-area. The demo may need updating soon.

openQuestions.title: Use position-area, not inset-area
openQuestions.detail: Sibling v131 feature `deprecation-of-css-anchor-positioning-property-inset-area` renames inset-area to position-area. This concept's submenu uses `inset-area: block-end span-inline-end` which will be deprecated/aliased. Switch to `position-area` with `inset-area` as a fallback for older Chrome.

openQuestions.title: Show what without-scope looks like
openQuestions.detail: The demo has no off-state — visitors only see the working case. Add a 'disable anchor-scope' toggle so the visitor sees all submenus snap to the third trigger when scope is removed.

summary: Strong demo of the canonical nav-menu pattern with a real fallback. Uses the (now-deprecated) `inset-area` keyword that's about to be renamed in this very milestone — should switch to `position-area`.
